comments.text: I would like to add a few references and partial results which seem directly relevant to this problem. Let \(\overline{\chi}(\mathbb R^2;m)=\max_{|A|=m}\chi(\mathbb R^2,A)\), where two points of \(\mathbb R^2\) are adjacent if their Euclidean distance belongs to \(A\). This is the corresponding infinite-graph formulation of the quantity \(L(m)\) in this problem; equivalently, by the de Bruijn--Erdős compactness theorem for graph colourings [dBE51, Theorem 1], it gives the same finite-subgraph supremum. First, Eric Naslund discussed this problem in an answer to a MathOverflow question of Matthew Kahle; see [MO20]. The usual lattice construction gives a lower bound \(\overline{\chi}(\mathbb R^2;m) \gg m\sqrt{\log m}\). On the other hand, the elementary product-colouring argument gives the general upper bound \(\overline{\chi}(\mathbb R^2;m)\le 7^m\). A related recent paper is Naslund's [Na23] (also available on arXiv: [Na23-arXiv]). For small \(m\), there is a concrete improvement when \(m=2\). Exoo and Ismailescu [EI19] proved that \(\chi(\mathbb R^2,\{1,2\})\ge 6\). Equivalently, in the notation of this problem, \(L(2)\ge 6\). Parts later gave a smaller and simpler 6-chromatic two-distance construction for a suitable pair of forbidden distances, based on a graph with only 16 vertices; see [Pa20]. References. [dBE51] N. G. de Bruijn and P. Erdős, ''A colour problem for infinite graphs and a problem in the theory of relations'', Proceedings of the Koninklijke Nederlandse Akademie van Wetenschappen, Series A 54 (1951), 371--373. [MO20] Matthew Kahle, ''Updated bounds or references for an old Erdős problem -- coloring the plane with multiple forbidden distances?'', MathOverflow question, with an answer by Eric Naslund, 2020--2022. [Na23] Eric Naslund, ''The chromatic number of \(\mathbb R^n\) with multiple forbidden distances'', Mathematika 69 (2023), 692--718. [EI19] Geoffrey Exoo and Dan Ismailescu, ''A 6-chromatic two-distance graph in the plane'', arXiv:1909.13177, 2019. [Pa20] Jaan Parts, ''A small 6-chromatic two-distance graph in the plane'', Geombinatorics 29/3 (2020), 111--115; arXiv:2010.12656.
